metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Anchor the first grep with a word boundary to prevent substring false-positives: replace `grep -Eiq "direct"` with `grep -Eiqw "direct"` (or `grep -Eiq "\bdirect\b"`) so it matches 'direct' as a word, not as a substring of 'indirect'.
evidence: grep -Eiq "direct" matches 'indirect', 'indirectly', 'direction', etc. — no word-boundary anchor
VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY item 1: substring false-positive — anchor the pattern with a word-boundary
findings.finding: AC1's first grep (`grep -Eiq "direct"`) matches the substring 'direct' inside words like 'indirect' or 'indirectly', so the command returns true even if the standalone word 'direct' never appears — a taxonomy item-1 false-positive that would let a file containing only 'indirect dependency' satisfy the criterion without ever asserting the edge 'must be DIRECT'.
scenarios: A writer adds only the sentence 'a transitive or indirect dependency does not count' — the first grep passes (matches 'indirect' containing 'direct') even though the word 'direct' as an independent concept was never added, yielding a green AC on a non-compliant doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for 'no other files changed', e.g. `[ "$(git diff --name-only origin/main | wc -l)" -eq 1 ]`, so the single-file constraint is mechanically verified.
evidence: AC2 reads: `git diff --name-only origin/main | grep -qxE "docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md"` and no other files changed
The `grep -qxE` command only confirms the doc IS in the diff; it does not assert the diff contains exactly one file
findings.finding: AC2's prose constraint 'no other files changed' is stated as a requirement but has no corresponding proving command — only the presence of the doc in the diff is verified, leaving the 'no other files' half of the criterion unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC2: prose 'and no other files changed'
scenarios: An executor inadvertently edits a second file (e.g. a CHANGELOG), AC2's git command still passes, and the 'no other files changed' constraint goes undetected as violated.
findings.suggested_fix: git diff --name-only origin/main | grep -qxE 'docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md' && [ "$(git diff --name-only origin/main | wc -l)" -eq 1 ]

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the Approach section explaining why docs-only is the right scope (e.g., 'the verifier already implements direct-edge-only checks correctly; only the documentation is incomplete, so a code change is unnecessary').
evidence: Plan says only 'Add one clause to the EXISTING citation section stating the edge must be DIRECT' with no stated reason why this scope (docs only) is preferred over also amending the error message in `unbacked_citations` or adding runtime transitive-dep detection.
The Grounding section confirms behavior accuracy ('direct is accurate to the shipped behavior') but this is framed as verification, not as a rationale for the chosen approach.
The doc being edited itself says under `## Approach`: 'name why the chosen approach beats the obvious alternative (G6)' — the plan's own approach section should model this.
findings.finding: The plan's Approach section states 'docs-only; no behavior change' but provides no positive rationale for WHY docs-only is the right scoping choice — it doesn't explain that the verifier's behavior is already correct and only the documentation is incomplete. Per G6, the absence of a positive rationale for the chosen approach is a finding.
scenarios: A plan reviewer or implementer reading this plan cannot understand why docs-only is chosen over also fixing the advisory error message to say 'direct edge required' — leaving scope ambiguity that could lead to under- or over-implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Approach section: 'Docs-only is correct because `_edge_verified` already implements direct-edge-only semantics; the verifier behavior needs no change, only the author-facing documentation needs to reflect it.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
